mappings.rationaleAI: Directly covers structures of all major body systems: skeleton, heart, nervous system, lymphatic system, abdomen, pelvis, and trunk (A through K).
Directly covers functions of all major body systems including cardiovascular, respiratory, digestive, endocrine, and nervous systems (A through K).
Examines the central nervous system, cerebral cortex, basal ganglia, sensory processing, and spinal cord architecture (J.1, J.2, J.3).
Directly covers blood components, haemoglobin, blood pressure, oxygen transport, and transfusion, corresponding to A.3 and A.4.
Details the lymphatic system including lymphocytes, antibody production, and immune defense mechanisms (B).
Directly covers thyroid, adrenal, pituitary, pancreatic islets, growth hormone, and reproductive hormones (E.1, E.2).
Directly covers the eye, retina, lens, optic nerve, colour vision, and visual processing (J.4).
Directly covers olfactory processes and the smell sense (J.6.e).
Explains cell biology underlying all body tissues, including blood cells, the nuclear envelope, and the endoplasmic reticulum (K).
Describes dental structures including canines, molars, enamel, and dentine, part of the mouth and digestive system (D.1.a).
Enzymes drive the digestive process, metabolic functions, and biochemical reactions throughout all body systems (D.2).
Illuminates the molecular basis of metabolism, cell membranes, and chemical bonding relevant to body fluids and tissues (K).
Addresses biochemical reactions, DNA, and cellular chemistry underlying the composition and function of body tissues and fluids (K).
Explains metabolism, vitamin function, and protein processing relevant to the digestive system's absorption and nutrient utilization (D.2).
Investigates pain perception and pain management, relevant to cutaneous senses including touch, heat, cold, and nociception (J.6.a).
Explains acoustics, frequency, pitch, and resonance relevant to the ear and the process of audition (J.5).
Analyzes the visual system, colour perception, motion perception, and the physiology of sensory processing (J.4, J.6).
Describes biological rhythms regulated by the suprachiasmatic nucleus of the hypothalamus and the pineal gland's melatonin secretion (E.1.d, E.1.h).
Shows how body structures differentiate during embryonic development, including nervous system formation and Hox gene patterning (J.1, H).
The gut microbiome is integral to digestive system function and nutrition absorption (D).
Explains the sympathetic nervous system, action potentials, spinal cord anatomy, and muscle physiology from an anaesthetic perspective (J.2, J.3, H.7).
Explores brain-based memory processes, including the hippocampus and long-term potentiation, relevant to the central nervous system's cognitive functions (J.1).
Applies fluid dynamics principles to blood circulation, respiratory airflow, and the physics of body fluid transport (A.4, C.3).
Takes a systems-level perspective on the digestive system and metabolic processes, modeling how organs interact as integrated networks (D, E).
Explores the sociology and cultural significance of the human body, providing broader context for understanding the structures and functions covered in this section.
